transcript: what makes wind air is all around us but we cannot see it we can feel it as it moves moving air is called wind but what makes the air move the answer is atmospheric pressure atmospheric pressure is the amount of force by weight that the air above is pressing down on a given point it can also be equivalent to temperature cold air is heavier or high pressure and hot air is lighter or low pressure a classroom demonstration of this can be done with an empty aquarium a lamp for heat some ice cubes and a smoke source heat up one end of the aquarium with the light source and cool the other end with the ice cubes this creates areas of high and low pressure place something on the aquarium that shields it from outside air then introduce some smoke so we can see the air moving kids don't do this without parental permission and assistance I've seen it done with a burning paper straw pinched at the end opposite the flame plastic straws don't work kids with the smoke in the aquarium we can see that the wind moves from the areas of high pressure to the areas of low pressure or the cool air moves to where the warm air is now think of the earth the equator is hotter than the poles the tilt of the earth in relation to the Sun changes as it orbits the earth and the friction of the air itself all affect the movement and creation of wind
